hero.description: An extraordinary gathering of faith, fire and fellowship. Come encounter the transforming power of God's grace.

paragraphs: What if the exhaustion you've been carrying isn't just physical?

It's been the kind that sleep doesn't fix. The kind that lingers even when life looks "fine" on the outside. You've kept going, building, working, showing up for others but somewhere deep down, there's a quiet knowing: something is missing.
Maybe you feel a void nothing around you seems to satisfy. A sense that there has to be more to this life.
And maybe that feeling isn't wrong.

What if one weekend could actually shift something on your inside? What if one weekend could bring answers to your curious mind? A weekend where the weight you've been carrying begins to lift. Where clarity replaces confusion, and where you reconnect with who your creator has called you to be.
Thousands gather for one reason: to encounter Jesus in a way that changes everything; In the way that will last a lifetime.

Over three transformative days, you'll step into an atmosphere charged with intense worship, powerful teaching, fervent prayer, and genuine community - the kind that reminds you that you were never meant to do life alone.
And this year, it's happening in Toronto, Canada.
Under the theme {theme}, drawn from Acts 4:33, this isn't just another gathering - it's an encounter.
We believe this: every person who shows up is one encounter away from a turning point.

Three days. 
One city. 
An encounter that could change everything.

verse.text: "And with great power the apostles gave witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus. And great grace was upon them all."

schedule.subtitle: All sessions are free and open to all registered attendees. Times are approximate and subject to change.
schedule.footnote: * Schedule is subject to change. All times are Eastern Time (ET).

main.name: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
main.title: Lead Pastor, Celebration Church International
main.bio: Apostle Emmanuel Iren is the visionary founder and lead pastor of Celebration Church International, a dynamic teaching ministry dedicated to spreading the gospel and helping people discover their purpose in Christ. With a passion for the Word of God and a heart for missions, Apostle Iren has led the church to establish Over 40 campuses across Nigeria, UK, Europe, Australia, Canada, and the USA, all while inspiring countless lives to grow in their faith and embrace the life-changing power of Christ.

aboutCity.heading: About Toronto
aboutCity.text: Toronto is Canada’s largest city and a global cultural hub. Known for its diversity, stunning skyline, and warm hospitality, Toronto is the perfect host city for Reboot Camp NA. The city is easily accessible from major North American cities via Pearson International Airport (YYZ) or Billy Bishop Airport (YTZ).

directions.title: By Subway (TTC)
steps: Transfer to Route 112 (West Mall) toward Attwell Dr
Rideshare or walk approx. 10 min to 272 Attwell Dr

directions.title: By Bus (TTC)
steps: Route 112 (West Mall) serves the Attwell Drive corridor
Route 37 (Islington) connects from Islington Subway Station
Check ttc.ca for real-time schedules and trip planner

directions.title: By Car / Rideshare
steps: From Hwy 401 W: Take Hwy 427 S, exit Dixon Rd E, follow to Attwell Dr
From downtown: Gardiner Expressway W → Hwy 427 N → Dixon Rd E

faqs.contactText: Can’t find your answer here? Email us at
faqs.stillHaveQsText: Our team is happy to help. Reach out and we’ll respond within 24–48 hours.

items.a: Yes! Registration and attendance at all sessions of Reboot Camp North America 2026 are completely free. There is no ticket price or entry fee. We simply ask that you register so we can plan effectively for the gathering.

Reboot Camp is open to all — believers, seekers, young and old, individuals and families. Whether you're a long-time Christian or just beginning your faith journey, you are warmly welcome. All nationalities are welcome.

items.q: Do I need to book accommodation in advance?
items.a: We strongly recommend booking accommodation early, especially for out-of-town attendees. Toronto is a busy city and accommodation near the venue can fill up quickly around our event dates. Check our Accommodation section for partner hotel rates.

items.q: Can I attend if I’m not a member of a specific church?
items.a: Absolutely. Reboot Camp is non-denominational. People from all churches, backgrounds and walks of life attend. You do not need to belong to any particular church or denomination.
Yes. Children’s sessions and supervised childcare will be available during key programme times. Please indicate your childcare needs on the registration form so we can make adequate arrangements.

items.q: Is there parking at the venue?
items.a: Yes, there is ample free parking available at the venue (500+ spots). For public transit users, the venue is accessible by TTC bus and subway. See our Venue section for detailed directions.

items.q: Will the sessions be recorded / livestreamed?
items.a: Yes! All main sessions will be recorded and available on our YouTube channel after the event. We also plan to livestream key evening sessions. Follow us on social media for livestream links closer to the event date.

Bring your Bible, a notebook, an open heart, and a faith expectation! The venue is air-conditioned, so you may want a light jacket. Comfortable clothing is encouraged. Food and beverages will be available for purchase on-site.
There is no strict dress code. We encourage smart casual or semi-formal attire, but what matters most is that you come with a heart ready to receive. Please dress modestly and comfortably.

items.q: How do I get a visa to travel to Canada?
items.a: If you are traveling from outside Canada and require a visa, please begin your application process immediately as visas can take several weeks to process. We are happy to provide a registration confirmation letter to support your visa application. Contact us at info@rebootcamp.ca.

There will be an opportunity to give during services, but giving is completely voluntary. No one will be pressured. You can also give online via our Giving section. All proceeds support the ministry and future camps.

items.q: How can I volunteer at Reboot Camp?
items.a: We would love to have you serve! To volunteer, please reach out to us via email at volunteers@rebootcamp.ca. Volunteer teams include ushers, hospitality, media, prayer, and children’s ministry.

giving.description: Reboot Camp is free to attend, but it takes significant resources to host. Your generous giving makes it possible for thousands to encounter God. Every gift, large or small, makes an eternal difference.

scripture.text: "Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver."

giving.trustNote: All online transactions are secured with SSL encryption. Receipts are available upon request. We are a registered non-profit and operate with full financial transparency.

options.title: Online Giving
options.description: Give securely online via credit/debit card, PayPal, or e-transfer. Quick and easy.

options.title: E-Transfer (Canada)
options.description: Send an Interac e-Transfer directly to our designated giving email address.

options.title: International Giving
options.description: For international supporters, use Wise or PayPal for currency-friendly transfers.
